old: 坐在我侧的人是我同学。
new: 他走到我侧，低声说了一句话。
reason: "坐在我侧" is unnatural; 侧 as standalone positional noun without directional modifier is incomplete.

old: The person sitting on my side is my classmate.
new: He walked to my side and said something quietly.
士兵保护着队长的左侧。
reason: "队长的侧" is grammatically incomplete; 侧 needs a directional modifier.

old: The soldiers protected the commander's flank.
new: The soldiers protected the commander's left flank.

old: 他很策略地避开了冲突。
new: 处理这种问题，他总是很有策略。
reason: "很策略地" is unnatural; 策略 used adverbially requires 有策略地 or 策略性地.

new: When handling this kind of issue, he is always very tactful.

old: 自行车的刹车坏了，很危险。
new: 进入弯道时，他及时刹车，避免了危险。
reason: Original uses 刹车 as a noun (brake mechanism), not the verb 'to brake' as defined.

old: The bicycle's brake is broken, which is dangerous.
new: When entering the curve, he braked in time and avoided danger.

old: He received the name of 'Best Player'.
new: He received the title of 'Best Player'.
reason: 称号 means an honorary/official title, not a generic 'name.'

old: This city received the name 'Civilized City'.
new: This city received the title 'Civilized City'.
reason: 称号 should be rendered as 'title,' not 'name.'
称号 should be rendered as 'title,' not 'name.'

old: Once the umbrella is opened to support it, it can block the rain.
new: Once the umbrella is propped open, it can block the rain.
reason: Translation was garbled; 伞撑开后可以挡雨 simply means 'after the umbrella is propped open, it can block the rain.'

old: An innumerable number of people attended this event.
new: Tens of thousands of people attended this event.
reason: 成千上万 means 'thousands upon thousands/tens of thousands,' not 'innumerable.'

old: There are innumerable cars in the city.
new: There are tens of thousands of cars in the city.
reason: Same mistranslation of 成千上万.

old: Innumerable tourists came here to visit.
new: Tens of thousands of tourists came here to visit.
reason: Same mistranslation of 成千上万.
